You're running an unsigned public beta of The Civic Desk. Windows SmartScreen may warn on install; that's expected. Report issues on GitHub.

1 ISSUE(S)
Warns
- Consider adding a source link

This passage looks like a factual claim without a linked source. Add a link if one exists, rewrite the claim, or leave it for the editor to approve.

Technical details
Press-freedom / legal-risk advisor

Advisory only. Run this when you want risk notes, verification paths, and wording options. The publisher decides what to investigate, edit, hold, or publish.

Run Advisor
Distinguish verified facts, allegations, opinion, and unanswered questions.
Use extra care with private individuals, minors, medical details, addresses, and active legal matters.
Keep notes on source reliability and why publishing serves the community.

"There are other types of actions (resolutions, appropriations, official legislative positions), but if you are interested in changing the law, it is best to first talk to the staff member who is an expert in that area, or  talk to your City Council member . Oftentimes, Council members direct staff to prepare an ordinance based on things they heard at their meetings from residents at the Public Invited to be Heard segment of the meeting.
 
For any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 303-651-8649.

textareas.value:
The city council will vote this week on a contract for roof work at the public library, impacting local government spending and construction timelines.

As outlined in the upcoming agenda [Source](evidence:13), the contract is part of essential maintenance efforts to ensure the library remains safe and functional. The total cost of the project has not yet been disclosed, but it will significantly affect city finances. Residents can expect potential changes to library hours during the construction period.

The public has an opportunity to provide input on this decision through the council's community engagement process [Source](evidence:13). Community members are encouraged to attend the meeting or submit comments in advance to ensure their voices are heard. The exact date and time of the vote will be updated as more information becomes available.

This vote is part of ongoing efforts to maintain public facilities, which are crucial for community engagement and education [Source](evidence:14). As construction timelines are established, further updates on library operations and access will be provided.

For more details, residents can review the full agenda packet or contact city officials directly. The council's next meeting is scheduled for this week; specific timing and location will be announced closer to the date [Source](evidence:13).

inputs.value: City Council to Vote on Roof Contract for Longmont Library
